objective: Add the governance-owned Stage D Remote MCP smoke/security proof runner, fixture e2e coverage, runbook closure rules, execution scope, and validation evidence for hldpro-governance#364 under epic #109.
scope_boundary: Modify only Remote MCP Stage D proof scripts, focused tests, runbook, issue #364 planning/evidence, and fixture proof artifacts.
Do not commit Cloudflare, JWT, HMAC, or endpoint secrets.
Do not claim live Stage D completion unless the live proof runner passes against a real remote endpoint with copied audit evidence.
Keep Stage A/B/C merged evidence intact and leave downstream local-ai-machine code unchanged.
out_of_scope: Installing or operating live cloudflared from this governance PR.
Closing hldpro-governance#109 without a live second-machine proof.
Adding secrets or live hostnames to repository files.
research_summary: Issue #109 Stage A merged in governance PR #360, and Stage B/C merged downstream in local-ai-machine PR #485. The remaining Stage D need is a repeatable remote-machine smoke/security proof. The current Stage A thin client targets an earlier JSON-RPC shape, while Stage B/C exposes `/mcp/call`, so this slice adds a Stage D runner for the merged bridge contract rather than changing downstream code.

sprints.name: Stage D proof harness
sprints.goal: Make Remote MCP Stage D live proof executable and CI-testable without exposing secrets.
tasks: Add Stage D smoke/security proof runner for the Stage B/C `/mcp/call` bridge contract.
Add fixture e2e mode that exercises the same acceptance checks and writes audit evidence.
Add tests for fixture e2e and live-mode fail-fast behavior.
Update the runbook with live env, commands, audit copy/verify procedure, and closure rules.
Record validation and execution scope evidence.
Run local tests, fixture e2e, audit verifier, local CI, and GitHub Actions.
acceptance_criteria: Proof runner fails fast when live Stage D config is missing.
Fixture e2e proves authenticated ping succeeds.
Fixture e2e proves anonymous request fails.
Fixture e2e proves spoofed origin is non-authoritative and rejected.
Fixture e2e proves PII-bearing request is rejected before dispatch.
Fixture e2e proves `lam.scrub_pii` remote call is rejected.
Fixture e2e writes audit JSONL and manifest evidence.
Audit verifier passes valid fixture evidence and fails a copied tamper sample.
Runbook states live Stage D closure requires real remote endpoint proof plus local stdio proof after tunnel stop.
Issue #109 remains open unless live proof passes.

specialist_reviews.focus: Stage D proof runner safety, secret handling, live/fixture distinction, audit verifier coverage, and runbook closure rules.
specialist_reviews.summary: Found live audit bypass risk from optional missing-audit flag and fixture args_hmac not binding request arguments. Both were fixed before PR publication, and re-review found no new material issues.

alternate_model_review.summary: This slice is deterministic Python/runbook/evidence work. The live remote proof remains an operator/environment run of the same harness.

execution_handoff.approved_scope_summary: Issue #364 Stage D proof harness may modify only Remote MCP proof scripts/tests, runbook, issue-specific planning/evidence, and fixture audit evidence while preserving #109 as the governing epic.
execution_handoff.next_execution_step: Run focused tests, fixture e2e, audit verifier, local CI, final review, then publish the proof-harness PR while leaving #109 open if live proof is not available.

material_deviation_rules: If live endpoint credentials are available, run live mode and record redacted evidence before closing #109.
If live endpoint credentials are unavailable, merge only the proof harness and do not close #109.
If the downstream bridge path changes from `/mcp/call`, update the runbook and tests in the same PR.
If fixture evidence begins to contain payloads or secrets, remove the artifact and replace it with redacted validation output.
